sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 16, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Two 3×3 green tokens with colored centers (white=active, charcoal=inactive). Arrow keys move the active token in 3-cell steps. MOUSE on a center swaps activation. Purple wall blocks movement. Two containers on left and right platforms. Goal: place tokens into containers.
- Plan: Move the active right token UP over the purple wall, RIGHT past it, then DOWN into the right container.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 15 of about 28 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Only the HUD bar changed at [63,54]. The token didn't move at all. So UP was a wasted move - the token is blocked from moving UP. The token at [30,27]-[32,29] can't move UP because there's no platform above it (the left platform is at [21,9]-[41,23] and the token is at col 27-29, which is on the gap bridge area).
Wait, actually the left platform is at [21,9]-[41,23]. The token is at [30,27]-[32,29]. Col 27-29 is outside the left platform (which ends at col 23). So the token is on the gap bridge [30,24]-[32,29], not on the left platform. That's why it can't move UP - there's no platform above the gap bridge.
So the token can only move horizontally on the gap bridge. To move vertically, it needs to be on one of the platforms.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics:
1. Tokens can move on connected surfaces (platforms and bridges)
2. The gap bridge only allows horizontal movement (it's only 1 cell tall in the vertical direction... wait, no, it's 3 rows tall: [30,24]-[32,29])
3. Actually, the bridge is 3 rows tall (rows 30-32) and 6 cols wide (cols 24-29)
Wait, let me reconsider. The left platform is [21,9]-[41,23]. The gap bridge is [30,24]-[32,29]. The right platform is [21,39]-[41,53]. The purple wall is [21,33]-[41,38].
So the platforms span rows 21-41, and the bridge spans rows 30-32. The bridge connects the left platform (cols 9-23) to the area before the purple wall (cols 24-29).
The token at [30,27]-[32,29] is on the bridge. It can move LEFT onto the left platform or RIGHT into the purple wall (blocked). It can't move UP or DOWN because... well, actually, the bridge is 3 rows tall, same as the token. If the token moves UP, it would go to [27,27]-[29,29], which is not on any platform or bridge. So it's blocked.
Similarly, if the token moves DOWN, it would go to [33,27]-[35,29], which is also not on any platform or bridge. So it's blocked.
So the token can only move horizontally on the bridge. To move vertically, it needs to be on one of the platforms.
Plan revision:
1. Move LEFT to get the token onto the left platform
2. On the left platform, move UP/DOWN to navigate
3. But the left container is at [32,11]-[36,15], which is on the left platform
4. The left token (inactive) is at [30,21]-[32,23], also on the left platform
Wait, but I need to figure out the actual goal. Let me think about what makes sense:
- There are 2 tokens and 2 containers
- The goal is probably to put each token into its corresponding container
- The left token needs to go to the left container
- The right token needs to go to the right container
For the left token to reach the left container:
- Left token: [30,21]-[32,23]
- Left container interior: [33,12]-[35,14]
- Need: LEFT and DOWN
For the right token to reach the right container:
- Right token: [30,27]-[32,29] (currently active)
- Right container interior: [27,45]-[29,47]
- Need: UP and RIGHT, but the purple wall blocks RIGHT
So the right token can't reach the right container directly. Maybe I need to:
1. First move the right token LEFT onto the left platform
2. Then activate the left token and move it into the left container
3. Then figure out how to get the right token to the right container
Or maybe the goal is different. Maybe I just need to put ONE token into ONE container? Or maybe the tokens need to be in specific containers based on their center colors?
Let me try a different approach: move the active token (right one) LEFT onto the left platform, then try to navigate it to the left container. If that works, I'll know the goal is to put tokens into containers.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The left token is at [30,21]-[32,23] and the left container is at [32,11]-[36,15]. The container is BELOW and LEFT of the token. So the left token needs to move LEFT and DOWN to reach the container.
The right token is at [30,27]-[32,29] and the right container is at [26,44]-[30,48]. The container is ABOVE and RIGHT of the token. So the right token needs to move UP and RIGHT to reach the container.
But the right token can't move RIGHT (blocked by purple wall) and can't move UP (no platform). So maybe the right token needs to go a different route?
Hmm, what if I move the right token LEFT onto the left platform, then UP, then... no, that doesn't help because the right container is on the right platform.
Wait, maybe the purple wall isn't a permanent blocker. Maybe clicking on it or doing something else changes it. Or maybe the tokens can pass through each other?
Let me try a different approach. Let me move the active token LEFT onto the left platform and see if it can then move UP or DOWN.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model revised: Token can only move on connected platform/bridge surfaces. UP/DOWN blocked on the narrow bridge. Need to get token onto a platform first for vertical movement. Let me move LEFT onto the left platform, then try vertical movement.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move LEFT to get token onto left platform, then try UP
# Token is at [30,27]-[32,29], left platform ends at col 23
# LEFT moves 3 cols, so: [30,27]-[32,29] -> [30,24]-[32,26] -> [30,21]-[32,23]
# But wait, left token is at [30,21]-[32,23], so we'd collide!
# Let's just try LEFT once
action(['LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
